pkg/clock.purpose: Process-global test clock replacing time.Now() with a controllable source backed by atomic drift/freeze vars. All production code must call clock.Now() so tests can freeze or shift time deterministically without interface injection.

patterns.name: clock.Now() everywhere
patterns.description: Production code must call clock.Now() instead of time.Now(). Package-level atomics mean no interface injection.

patterns.name: FreezeTime/UnFreeze in tests
patterns.description: Freeze with clock.FreezeTime(t) and restore with defer clock.UnFreeze() or defer clock.ResetTime().

patterns.name: Monotonic clock stripped
patterns.description: clock.Now() always calls .Round(0) to strip the monotonic reading, preventing subtle comparison bugs when storing/comparing times.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package: atomic drift int64 for SetTime, atomic frozen int32 + atomic.Value for FreezeTime. SetTime computes drift as time.Since(t).Nanoseconds().
key_file_guides.watch_for: drift is the offset subtracted from wall time, not an absolute time. FreezeTime and SetTime are independent mechanisms — FreezeTime short-circuits drift entirely.

anti_patterns: Calling time.Now() directly in production code — always use clock.Now().
Forgetting defer clock.ResetTime() / clock.UnFreeze() in tests — leaks frozen/drifted state across cases.
Adding a context parameter or Clock interface — the global atomic design is intentional for zero-overhead usage.

decisions.decision: Package-level atomics instead of an injected Clock interface.
decisions.rationale: Zero call-site changes across the codebase; atomic ops are lock-free and safe for concurrent test use.

pkg/cmpx.purpose: Single-file package exposing a Comparable[T] constraint and a Compare[T] generic function for deterministic ordering of domain types, without boilerplate type assertions.

patterns.name: Implement Comparable[T] on domain types
patterns.description: Any type needing ordering implements Compare(T) int; the generic Compare[T] function dispatches to left.Compare(right).
patterns.example: func Compare[T Comparable[T]](left, right T) int { return left.Compare(right) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Comparable[T] interface and Compare[T] generic helper. No dependencies beyond stdlib.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Intentionally minimal — do not add non-ordering utilities here.

anti_patterns: Adding any logic beyond comparison ordering to this package.
Using reflect.DeepEqual or sort.Slice comparisons in callers instead of implementing Comparable[T].

pkg/contextx.purpose: Context-keyed key-value store (via peterbourgon/ctxdata) plus a slog.Handler adapter that auto-attaches stored attributes to every log record emitted within a request context. Propagates structured log fields through service layers without threading explicit logger parameters.

patterns.name: WithAttr / WithAttrs to annotate context
patterns.description: Call contextx.WithAttr(ctx, key, value) or WithAttrs(ctx, map) to attach structured data; a ctxdata bag is lazily created if absent.

patterns.name: NewLogHandler wraps existing slog.Handler
patterns.description: Wrap any slog.Handler with contextx.NewLogHandler to add context attributes to every record. Install at logger construction time, not per-request.

key_file_guides.role: WithAttr/WithAttrs write to a ctxdata bag (created via ctxdata.New if none exists); both silently ignore Set errors.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ctxdata.From returns nil if no bag exists — the helpers handle nil, but raw ctxdata callers must too.

key_file_guides.role: Handler wraps a slog.Handler; Handle reads ctxdata.GetAllMap and appends all entries as slog.Any attributes before delegating.
key_file_guides.watch_for: WithAttrs and WithGroup must re-wrap the inner handler in a new Handler struct to preserve contextx behaviour on derived loggers.

anti_patterns: Using context.WithValue directly for structured log fields instead of contextx.WithAttr.
Installing contextx.NewLogHandler more than once in a handler chain — double-attaches the same attributes.

decisions.decision: peterbourgon/ctxdata as the context bag instead of custom context keys.
decisions.rationale: Provides a typed map API accessible from any layer without defining package-private key types everywhere.

pkg/convert.purpose: Nil-safe generic pointer and type conversion helpers used throughout the codebase to eliminate repetitive nil-guard boilerplate when mapping between domain, API, and Ent-generated types.

patterns.name: ToPointer for value-to-pointer
patterns.description: Use convert.ToPointer[T](value) instead of taking the address of a local variable when the intent is nil-safety.

patterns.name: MapToPointer / SliceToPointer return nil on empty
patterns.description: Both return nil (not a pointer to an empty collection) when input is empty. Prefer these over lo.EmptyableToPtr for maps and slices.

patterns.name: SafeDeRef for nil-safe pointer transformation
patterns.description: Applies a mapping function only if the pointer is non-nil; returns nil otherwise. Replaces repetitive if-ptr-nil blocks in converter files.

patterns.name: ToStringLike for type-aliased string conversions
patterns.description: Converts *Source to *Dest where both are ~string kind, avoiding unsafe casts.

key_file_guides.role: All pointer/type helpers: ToPointer, MapToPointer, SliceToPointer, ToStringLike, SafeDeRef, SafeToUTC, DerefHeaderPtr (for *map/*slice), StringerPtrToStringPtr.
key_file_guides.watch_for: lo.EmptyableToPtr is explicitly avoided for maps/slices — use MapToPointer/SliceToPointer for nil-on-empty semantics.

key_file_guides.role: TimePtrIn converts *time.Time to a given location, nil-safe; returns a new pointer without mutating in place.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns nil when input is nil — callers must not assume a non-nil result.

anti_patterns: Using lo.EmptyableToPtr on maps or slices — it does not treat empty collections as nil.
Adding domain-specific conversion logic here — this package is for generic pointer/type mechanics only.
Taking the address of a local variable instead of convert.ToPointer when the intent is nil-safety.

pkg/currencyx.purpose: Thin ISO 4217 currency wrapper on invopop/gobl/currency adding a Calculator for currency-precision rounding and largest-remainder allocation helpers. Used across billing and subscription for all monetary arithmetic to ensure correct subunit rounding per currency.

patterns.name: Validate before Calculator
patterns.description: Always call Code.Validate() or obtain a Calculator via Code.Calculator() (which validates internally). Never use a Code value without validation.
patterns.example: calc, err := currencyx.Code("USD").Calculator(); if err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Calculator.RoundToPrecision for monetary amounts
patterns.description: Round alpacadecimal.Decimal to the correct subunit count for the currency. Never hardcode decimal places.
patterns.example: rounded := calc.RoundToPrecision(amount) // USD -> 2 places, JPY -> 0 places

patterns.name: Largest-remainder allocation at currency precision
patterns.description: Use AllocateByWeight (WeightedAllocationInput) or AllocateByAmount (AmountAllocationInput) to split an amount across keys; both round at the currency precision and distribute the remainder by largest fractional remainder, with CompareKey as a deterministic tie-breaker.
patterns.example: out, err := currencyx.AllocateByWeight(calc, currencyx.WeightedAllocationInput[string]{Amount: total, Items: items, CompareKey: cmp.Compare})

key_file_guides.role: Code type (alias of currency.Code), Validate, Calculator constructor, RoundToPrecision using Def.Subunits, IsRoundedToPrecision.
key_file_guides.watch_for: RoundToPrecision uses Def.Subunits (not smallestDenomination) — correct for online payments; physical-cash rounding may differ.

key_file_guides.role: Generic largest-remainder allocation: WeightedAllocationItem/AmountAllocationItem inputs and AllocateByWeight/AllocateByAmount returning per-key amounts that sum exactly to the input amount.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Zero input amount returns nil; CompareKey ties are broken deterministically — supply it (e.g. cmp.Compare) when item order is not stable.

anti_patterns: Rounding monetary amounts with a fixed precision constant instead of Calculator.RoundToPrecision.
Using currency.Code (gobl) directly instead of currencyx.Code — the wrapper adds Validate() and Calculator().
Hand-splitting amounts proportionally instead of AllocateByWeight/AllocateByAmount — manual splits drop the rounding remainder.

decisions.decision: Wraps gobl/currency rather than implementing ISO 4217 from scratch.
decisions.rationale: GOBL is already a transitive dependency for invoicing; reusing its currency definitions avoids a duplicate definition table.

code_examples.scenario: Round a billing amount to currency precision
